title: Immunhistochemistry (Stadelmann Lab - single antibody, DAB-POX-Avidin-Biotin)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5thif","text":"This protocol describes staining of any primary antibody using a biotin coupled secondary antibody using Avidin coupled peroxidase.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Immunohistochemistry was performed on 2-3 µm thick paraffin sections.
Blocking in 10% normal goat serum in PBS for 10 min @ RT
Decant 10% NGS in PBS and move directly to the next step without washing

Incubation of primary antibodies over night with respective dilution in 10% NGS @RT in a humid chamber target antigen - human host species Clone Company Catalog Number Dilution 1 pTau mouse at8 Thermo Fisher Scientific MN1020 1:100 Selected Antibodies
3x PBS or optional 3x TBS -> move to AP coupled secondary staining step 9 (dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3byl4952rgo5/v1 )
Slides were developed using DAB (1gin40mL PBS -> 1mL) 1 mL Aliquots + 50 mL PBS + 20 µL 30% H2O2)
secondary antibody Incubation with secondary antibody coupled to biotin at RT for 1h in a humid chamber %s %s
ExtrAvidin-peroxidase 1:1000 in 10% NGS for 1h in humid chamber at RT %s %s
heat antigen retrieval (1x citrate buffer 10 mM , pH 6) - 30 min steamer adopt according to tissue and antibody
formic acid (98%) (for Amyloid beta immunhistochemistry) - 1minute at RT
3% hydrogen peroxide in PBS 10 min @ RT
creator: Jonas Franz
